disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Long-standing background of blackheads/whiteheads and oily skin on face and upper trunk
trigger_question_examples: Have you had skin problems like spots or oily skin before this flare?
Do you get blackheads or whiteheads normally?
Is this on a background of ongoing spots and oily skin?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, I've always had oily skin and spots on my face and upper back — this is just way worse than usual.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Only a mildly raised temperature; no high fever or shaking chills
trigger_question_examples: Have you had a fever or shaking chills?
Any feeling hot and cold or rigors?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I checked and it was 37.6. I feel a bit run down but no shaking chills or high fever.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I don't know, I feel hot in the face but that's the spots.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Redness is confined around the cysts; not spreading toward the eyes and no visual change
trigger_question_examples: Is the redness spreading up toward your eyes or eyelids?
Any change in your vision or eye pain?
Is the red area getting bigger and moving across your face?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, the redness is just around the lumps on my cheeks. My eyes are fine and my vision is normal.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: It's all red, I can't really tell.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Recent high stress with increased oily skin preceded the flare
trigger_question_examples: Has anything changed recently — stress, products, routine?
Was there anything before the flare started?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've been really stressed lately and my skin got much oilier just before this started.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Is this a serious skin infection? The nurse on the phone said it might be spreading.
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Acknowledge the concern and screen for true cellulitis/periorbital spread (fever, spreading erythema, visual change) rather than either dismissing or assuming infection. Should not commit to 'cellulitis' without the screening negatives, nor dismiss the pain.
